subject: Project Management
title: No Project Manager in Scrum
description: One-minute skill: No Project Manager in Scrum.

hook.text: Scrum has no project manager role. The responsibilities are split across the three roles.

buildup.text: The Product Owner handles 'what.' The team handles 'how.' The Scrum Master handles the process.

discovery.text: This was intentional — centralizing control in one person created bottlenecks and micromanagement.

twist.text: Many organizations keep the PM title and bolt Scrum on top — creating role confusion and power struggles.

climax.text: Scrum doesn't kill the PM — it distributes the PM's work to people closer to the problem.

punchline.text: The role didn't vanish. It was shared.

quiz.question: Why does Scrum not include a project manager role?
options: To distribute responsibilities and avoid bottlenecks
Because PMs are not needed in software
